L1.area: Action execution pipeline and external trigger handling

L2.description: An OS command injection vulnerability exists where certain argument types bypass shell safety checks, allowing shell metacharacters to be injected into commands executed via sh -c. Additionally, external trigger payloads can inject arbitrary key-value pairs that skip type safety validation entirely before being interpolated into shell commands, enabling unauthenticated remote code execution.

L3.area: Action execution pipeline and external trigger handling
L3.description: An OS command injection vulnerability exists where certain argument types bypass shell safety checks, allowing shell metacharacters to be injected into commands executed via sh -c. Additionally, external trigger payloads can inject arbitrary key-value pairs that skip type safety validation entirely before being interpolated into shell commands, enabling unauthenticated remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The shell safety check does not block the password argument type, allowing shell metacharacter injection via sh -c. Webhook-extracted JSON values also skip type safety checks entirely, enabling unauthenticated command injection.
